# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

class ApplicationMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com")
  layout "mailer"
end

class SendgridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  default delivery_method_options: {
    api_key: ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"],
    domain: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_DOMAIN", "localhost")
  }

  def send_email(to:, subject:, body:, from: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com"), cc: nil, bcc: nil, reply_to: nil)
    mail_options = {
      to: Array(to),
      from: from,
      subject: subject,
      reply_to: reply_to
    }

    mail_options[:cc] = Array(cc) if cc.present?
    mail_options[:bcc] = Array(bcc) if bcc.present?

    mail(mail_options) do |format|
      format.text { render plain: body.to_s }
      format.html { render html: body.to_s.html_safe }
    end
  end
end

Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
  config.action_mailer.default_options = {
    from: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com")
  }

  if ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"].present?
    config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :sendgrid_actionmailer
    config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {}
    config.action_mailer.sendgrid_actionmailer_settings = {
      api_key: ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"]
    }
  else
    config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
    config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
      address: ENV.fetch("SMTP_ADDRESS", "smtp.sendgrid.net"),
      port: ENV.fetch("SMTP_PORT", 587).to_i,
      domain: ENV.fetch("SMTP_DOMAIN", "localhost"),
      authentication: :plain,
      user_name: ENV.fetch("SMTP_USERNAME", "apikey"),
      password: ENV.fetch("SMTP_PASSWORD", ""),
      enable_starttls_auto: true,
      open_timeout: ENV.fetch("SMTP_OPEN_TIMEOUT", 5).to_i,
      read_timeout: ENV.fetch("SMTP_READ_TIMEOUT", 5).to_i
    }
  end
end
